qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Backup restore creates watches with unvalidated URLs and properties, bypassing is_safe_valid_url enforcement
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding identifies URL validation bypass in backup restore path (changedetectionio/blueprint/backups/restore.py), while advisory describes jq env builtin information disclosure in html_tools.py:380-388. Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different security boundary.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes jq env builtin information disclosure in changedetectionio/html_tools.py:380-388, where jq: and jqraw: include filter expressions allow the env builtin to leak process environment variables. Finding 'backup-restore-unvalidated-watch-injection' identifies a different vulnerability in the same package (URL validation bypass in backup restore path at changedetectionio/blueprint/backups/restore.py), but this is a distinct security boundary violation with different root cause. The finding does not reference html_tools.py, jq filter expressions, or the env builtin restriction bypass.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable file: changedetectionio/html_tools.py:380-388 (jq filter expression compilation and execution)
Advisory root cause: No restriction on dangerous jq builtins, specifically the env builtin which reads all process environment variables
Finding root cause: Missing is_safe_valid_url() call in restore path, allowing unvalidated URLs
No finding references html_tools.py, jq: filters, jqraw: filters, or env builtin
